sources.title: 2026 Iran war ceasefire - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: On June 11, Trump announced that a ceasefire for further 60 days was reached. Trump says Vance to attend US-Iran deal signing likely in Europe this weekend.

sources.title: 2026 Iran war - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Since 28 February 2026, the United States and Israel have been at war with Iran and its regional allies. Active hostilities broke out after surprise US-Israeli airstrikes.

sources.title: 2025-2026 Iran-United States negotiations - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: On April 12, 2025, Iran and the United States began a series of negotiations aimed at reaching a nuclear peace agreement. Venues included Muscat, Rome, Geneva, and Islamabad.

sources.title: Trump says Vance to attend US-Iran deal signing likely in Europe this weekend
sources.snippet: Trump says Vance to attend US-Iran deal signing likely in Europe this weekend (referenced in Wikipedia ceasefire article)

sources.title: Trump says agreement with Iran over end of war is 'largely negotiated'
sources.snippet: Trump stated that a memorandum of understanding was close to finalisation and suggested the Strait of Hormuz could soon reopen under a ceasefire framework.

sources.title: U.S. and Iran hold high-stakes nuclear talks in Geneva as threat of war looms
sources.snippet: US and Iran held nuclear talks in Geneva in February 2026 against backdrop of military threat.

key_facts: On June 11, 2026, Trump announced a 60-day ceasefire extension with Iran to negotiate all contentious issues for a final agreement
Trump stated that VP JD Vance would attend a 'US-Iran deal signing likely in Europe this weekend' (around June 14-15, 2026)
Geneva hosted Round 2 of US-Iran negotiations on February 15, 2026, making it the most likely European venue for the signing
The 2026 Iran war began on February 28, 2026, when the US and Israel launched surprise airstrikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
Iran retaliated by striking Israel, US bases, and Gulf states, and by closing the Strait of Hormuz, causing the largest oil supply disruption in history
The initial two-week ceasefire was agreed on April 8, 2026, mediated by Pakistan
The Islamabad Talks (April 11-12, 2026) failed to produce a lasting agreement
Trump extended the ceasefire indefinitely on April 21, 2026, while maintaining a naval blockade on Iran
On May 24, 2026, Trump said a memorandum of understanding with Iran was close to finalization
Key US negotiators include Special Envoy Steve Witkoff, Special Envoy Jared Kushner, VP JD Vance, and CENTCOM commander Brad Cooper
Key Iranian negotiators include FM Abbas Araghchi and Deputy FM Majid Takht-Ravanchi; Ali Larijani was assassinated during the war
Pakistan (Army Chief Asim Munir) served as the primary mediator throughout the negotiations
As of June 15, 2026, no confirmed reports that the Geneva signing ceremony has been completed
